turns.student: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
turns.patient: Hi, thanks for seeing me. I've been having some really bad abdominal pain and bloody stools for about three weeks now. It's been getting worse over time.

turns.student: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
turns.patient: The pain is a 7 or 8 out of 10 most of the time, and it's mainly crampy in my lower abdomen, especially on the left side. The bloody stools have also increased in frequency – I'm having around 8-10 bowel movements per day, and they're mostly loose to watery with mucus. It's really affecting my work performance because I'm always worried about where the next bathroom is.

turns.student: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
turns.patient: To be honest, besides the pain and diarrhea, I've also been feeling pretty fatigued lately. It's hard to keep up with my usual workload, even though I'm working long hours, and it feels like I'm just going through the motions most days.

turns.student: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
turns.patient: No, I don't really get sweaty or feel like I'm going to pass out. Sometimes I might feel a bit lightheaded if I haven't eaten in a while, but it's not usually related to my bowel movements.

turns.student: Thank you for sharing that. I really appreciate your honesty. Does the discomfort stay in one place or does it spread anywhere?
turns.patient: The pain and discomfort are mostly localized to my lower abdomen, especially on the left side where it feels like a cramp. Occasionally, it might radiate up into my back or down towards my groin, but it's usually still focused in that one area.

turns.student: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
turns.patient: To be honest, besides the abdominal pain and diarrhea, I've also been experiencing some achy pain in both my knees and ankles, but no swelling or redness. It's not too bad, just a dull ache that comes and goes. And like I said before, the fatigue is really getting to me – it's affecting my work and personal life.

turns.student: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
turns.patient: The pain and discomfort are mostly localized to my lower abdomen, especially on the left side where it feels like a cramp. Occasionally, it might radiate up into my back or down towards my groin, but it's usually still focused in that one area. As for swelling, I haven't noticed anything significant – just the achy joints and fatigue.

turns.student: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
turns.patient: Actually, yeah, sometimes I do get a bit queasy or feel nauseous after eating, especially if I eat something greasy or spicy. But it's not all the time – just occasionally when my symptoms are particularly bad. And like I mentioned earlier, ibuprofen seems to make the abdominal pain worse, so I've been trying to avoid taking that for now.

turns.student: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
turns.patient: To be honest, my energy's been pretty low lately. The fatigue seems to be getting worse, and it's hard to motivate myself to do even simple things, let alone go for walks or exercise like I used to. As for appetite, I've actually lost a bit of weight over the past three weeks – about 4 kilograms (9 pounds) – because eating doesn't really appeal to me when my stomach's been feeling this bad.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
turns.patient: I haven't had any significant fevers. But, I have noticed that I've been waking up a few times in the middle of the night because of the nocturnal diarrhea – it's really uncomfortable and disrupts my sleep. And, to be honest, sometimes I do feel a bit cold when I'm sleeping, but I don't know if that's related to the diarrhea or just stress from everything else going on.

turns.student: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
turns.patient: I have a history of appendicitis, which I had removed when I was 16 years old. Other than that, I don't have any known significant medical conditions. My parents are pretty healthy too - my mom is fine and my dad has type 2 diabetes, but he's been managing it well for years now.

turns.student: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
turns.patient: I've actually been self-medicating with loperamide (Imodium) for diarrhea over the past two weeks, but it's only provided minimal relief. I've also been trying to manage my pain and inflammation with ibuprofen, but like I mentioned earlier, that seems to make things worse.

turns.student: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
turns.patient: To be honest, my eating habits have been pretty irregular lately due to work and stress. I usually grab whatever's quick and easy because cooking or meal planning just feels like too much effort when my stomach's been feeling this bad. It's mostly takeout or fast food, which isn't great for me, but it's what I can manage right now.

turns.student: It sounds like your life keeps you really busy. Do you get a chance to exercise or stay active?
turns.patient: Honestly, exercising has been out of the question lately due to my symptoms and fatigue. Even just getting through a day feels like a chore, let alone trying to fit in any physical activity. I've missed going for walks with my girlfriend because the diarrhea makes it hard to leave the house without knowing where the next bathroom is, and I don't want to risk having an accident.

turns.student: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was 30. She's been managing it for a while now, but it's always something that worries me because we're close and I know how hard it can be to deal with chronic illness.

turns.student: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
turns.patient: Yes, I do worry a bit about my own health because of my sister's experience with Crohn's disease. It's hard not to think about the possibility of something similar happening to me, especially since we're genetically related and share some similar symptoms. But at the same time, I try not to jump to conclusions or assume that I have the same thing just because of our family history.

turns.student: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
turns.patient:
I'm a bit worried about my health, especially since my sister has Crohn's disease. But at the same time, I try not to jump to conclusions or assume that I have the same thing just because of our family history.

As for drinking alcohol, I do occasionally drink beer with friends or on weekends. To be honest, I've actually increased my consumption a bit over the past month due to stress from my symptoms and work. Normally, I'd say 2-3 beers most weeknights, but lately, it's been more like 4-5 per night. It helps me wind down and feel a bit better temporarily, but I know it's not healthy.

Coping with stress has been tough for me lately. I usually try to stay busy at work or watch TV/movies in the evenings, but sometimes that doesn't even help. Lately, I've found myself just trying to get through each day without too much thought about the future or what might happen next. It's not a great feeling, and I know it's not sustainable.

turns.student: I want to make sure we're being thorough. Do you smoke or use any substances?
turns.patient: No, I don't smoke and I've never used recreational drugs. I've been trying to stay focused on managing my symptoms and getting through each day without too much added stress.

turns.student: Is there anything you've been hesitant to tell me? I promise there's no judgment here.
turns.patient:
I've been feeling really overwhelmed and worried about my health, especially with the family history of Crohn's disease. It's hard for me not to think about it when I'm experiencing these symptoms. I've also been feeling a bit anxious about how much ibuprofen I've been taking, even though it makes the pain worse - I just don't know what else to do sometimes.

And yeah, I guess I have been drinking more beer than usual lately, partly because of stress and partly as a way to try and cope. It's not something I'm proud of, but it feels like a temporary escape from everything going on.

turns.student: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
turns.patient:
I've been feeling really anxious about my health because of my family history with Crohn's disease. It's hard not to think about the possibility of having something similar, especially since I'm experiencing these symptoms. I've also been worried about how long it'll take to get some answers and figure out what's going on. Just knowing that I have a name for what's wrong and can start treating it would be a huge relief.

And yeah, I guess I have been drinking more beer than usual lately, partly because of stress and partly as a way to try and cope. It's not something I'm proud of, but it feels like a temporary escape from everything going on.
